title: Internal Error: attempted to destroy locked Thread Pool
body:
I'm seeing flaky failures in ci using verilator 5.020. The failure does not appear to be tied to any specific test, because rerunning typically succeeds. It looks like a race condition in shutdown.

```console 
INFO     cocotb:simulator.py:302 %Error: Internal Error: attempted to destroy locked Thread Pool
INFO     cocotb:simulator.py:302 %Error: Verilator aborted. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
INFO     cocotb:simulator.py:302 %Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; \
    exec verilator_bin -cc --exe -Mdir tb/axis/axis_id/sim_build/test_axis_id \
    -DCOCOTB_SIM=1 \
    --top-module axis_id \
    --vpi \
    --public-flat-rw \
    --prefix Vtop \
    -o axis_id \
    -LDFLAGS -Wl\,-rpath\,/nix/store/bvcp6l3g4ac52gv3fjl8ig1dyfwwhczv-python3-3.9.13-env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb/libs\ -L/nix/store/bvcp6l3g4ac52gv3fjl8ig1dyfwwhczv-python3-3.9.13-env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb/libs\ -lcocotbvpi_verilator \   
    -DOBSOLETE \
    -DONESPIN \
    -Wno-fatal \
    --assert -DFORMAL \
    --coverage-line \
    -j 0 \
    -O3 \
    -CFLAGS -O3 \
    --threads 24 \
    /nix/store/bvcp6l3g4ac52gv3fjl8ig1dyfwwhczv-python3-3.9.13-env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb/share/lib/verilator/verilator.cpp \
    .verilator.vlt \
    rtl/axis/axis_id.sv
```

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

The above example is a performed with this cutdown example. Its being exercised with cocotb, using cocotbaxi-ext library. I can provide a tb is required, but everything I'm seeing suggests this isn't related to the actual test

```systemverilog
module axis_id (
    input logic aclk,
    input logic aresetn,
    
    input logic s_axis_tvalid,
    output logic s_axis_tready,
    input logic s_axis_tdata,
    input logic s_axis_tlast,
    
    output logic m_axis_tvalid,
    input logic m_axis_tready,
    output logic m_axis_tlast,
    output logic m_axis_tdata
);

always_comb begin
    m_axis_tvalid = s_axis_tvalid;
    m_axis_tdata = s_axis_tdata;
    m_axis_tlast = s_axis_tlast;
    s_axis_tready = m_axis_tready;
end

endmodule
```
